contentBlocks.title: Why convert GIF to PNG?
contentBlocks.html:
GIF is limited to 256 colours and uses a dated compression algorithm. PNG supports millions of colours, alpha-channel transparency, and more efficient lossless compression.

Converting GIF to PNG is useful when you need a static image from an animated GIF, better colour depth, or when a platform requires PNG uploads.

Note: this tool extracts the first frame of animated GIFs. If you need to preserve animation, consider a video format instead.

contentBlocks.title: When should you convert GIF to PNG?

items.title: Static thumbnails
items.description: Extract a single frame from an animated GIF to use as a thumbnail or preview.

items.title: Better colour quality
items.description: GIF is limited to 256 colours. PNG supports full 24-bit colour for richer images.

items.title: Documents
items.description: Insert static PNG images instead of animated GIFs that may not play in all readers.

items.title: Web graphics
items.description: PNG with alpha transparency is often a better choice than GIF for static web graphics.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about GIF to PNG conversion

items.question: Does this convert the animation?
items.answer: No. This tool extracts the first frame of the GIF as a static PNG image.

items.question: Is quality lost?
items.answer: No. PNG uses lossless compression. The extracted frame is pixel-perfect.

items.question: Is transparency preserved?
items.answer: Yes. GIF transparency is carried over to the PNG alpha channel.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
Are files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs locally in your browser.

items.question: What is the maximum file size?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported.
